accepted_claims.text:
RLM-FORGE presents RLM as a deployable runtime primitive in an existing agent runtime, not as a new model architecture.
Chunk 001 cites abstract lines 42-54 and contribution lines 108-117; chunk 004 cites conclusion lines 970-975.

RLM-FORGE presents RLM as a deployable runtime primitive in an existing agent runtime, not as a new model architecture.
Chunk 001 cites abstract lines 42-54 and contribution lines 108-117; chunk 004 cites conclusion lines 970-975.

The architecture separates recursive orchestration from bounded inner inference: Ouroboros owns recursion and trace/control state, Hermes performs bounded local calls, and TraceGuard decides whether parent synthesis can be committed.
Chunk 002 cites lines 241-242, 280-291, and 311-313.

RLM-FORGE preserves operational RLM control surfaces at runtime: bounded sub-calls, explicit parent-child call graphs, evidence handles, replayable outputs, provider portability, and evidence-gated synthesis.
Chunk 001 cites lines 42-54 and 114-117; chunk 002 cites lines 246-270 and 272-275; chunk 004 cites lines 970-975.

Hermes built-in memory and RLM-FORGE guarded memory are treated as separable runtime layers with different roles.
Chunk 001 cites lines 63-68 and 143-150; chunk 003 cites lines 524-535; chunk 004 cites lines 571-575 and 984-985.
Abstract and contribution framing for runtime-lifted RLM, preserved control surfaces, TraceGuard evidence discipline, portability contract-pass claims, and bounded operational memory.

Architecture and response-contract claims: Ouroboros owns control and trace state, Hermes executes bounded inner calls, TraceGuard controls parent commit, and the handoff/response envelope is validated before state mutation.

Operational-memory experiment: allowlisted JSONL operational records, memory_priors excluded from accepted evidence, fresh evidence manifests rebuilt from current child outputs, 24-cell memory-enabled matrix claim, Hermes built-in memory observation, and deterministic runtime-control benchmark framing.

Memory-runtime benchmark details, contamination robustness, layered-memory ablation, adaptive repair memory, and conclusion claims about runtime-lifted RLM, provider portability, live contract passing, and fresh-evidence enforcement under memory.
